page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as evidenced by weak multi-core scores in stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block reads are average, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the bottom 10% of tested systems, measuring 20.8 GB/sec and 11.76 GB/sec respectively. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, whereas database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving fall into the lower 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highmem-4 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its low vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is well-suited for light database caching, single-threaded data processing, small-scale LLM inference, and general-purpose tasks requiring a larger memory footprint than standard instances. It is less suitable for high-throughput web serving, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale database operations due to its constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core performance.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on 2 physical cores with multithreading, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with large-block memory writes limited to 11.76 GB/sec. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive, low-concurrency workloads. It is best suited for light database caching, text processing, and small-scale language model inference, while being less optimal for high-throughput database operations or intensive multi-threaded compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, designed for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and strong single-core performance for memory-sensitive, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM providing a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Constrained uncached memory bandwidth under heavy large-block transfers
Best suited for light database caching, text processing, and small LLM inference
